build.title: BUILD YOUR PC
build.description: Design your PC using our simple builder. Choose from components available in the Latvian market, all with live, accurate pricing.

auto.title: MAKE THE BUILDING EASY
auto.description: Automatically complete your build at any stage using our smart auto-builder.

share.title: SHARE YOUR BUILDS
share.description: Browse community builds, get inspired, and share your own.

builderSection.title: Building Your First PC
builderSection.step1: 1. Go to the <buildLink>Build</buildLink> page by clicking the <buildButton>Build</buildButton> button in the navigation bar.
builderSection.step2: 2. Find the component list section and click the <addButton></addButton> button to add a new component.
builderSection.step3: 3. Use the search bar and filters to browse and find components that match your preferences.
builderSection.step3Note: Incompatible components will be marked with an "Incompatible" flag.
builderSection.step4: 4. Select a component from the list and click <selectText>Select</selectText> to add it to your build. It will then appear in the build info panel and the component list.
builderSection.step4Note: You can remove a component or view it in the store using the buttons at the bottom of the component card.
builderSection.step5: 5. Repeat these steps until your build is complete.
builderSection.step6: 6. Make sure to name your build and add the optional notes by filling out their fields.
builderSection.step7: 7. Once you’ve selected all your components and named your build, click the <saveButtonText>Save Build</saveButtonText> button in the build info panel.
builderSection.step7Note: You can clear the entire build or remove individual components by clicking the Clear Build button or pressing the <closeIcon></closeIcon> icon next to each component.

autoSection.title: Using the Automatic Builder
autoSection.step1: 1. Optionally, start by adding any components you want included in your build. You can also use the automatic component generator under the filters to find a compatable component automatically.
autoSection.step1Note: This step is optional. You can skip it to generate a fully automatic build.
autoSection.step2: 2. Scroll to the bottom of the build information panel and select the <autoBuilderText>Auto Builder</autoBuilderText> option.
autoSection.step3: 3. Set your budget using the slider, or choose the unlimited budget option to get the best possible components.
autoSection.step3Note: The budget applies to the entire build. If you have already selected components, their cost will be deducted from your available budget.
autoSection.step4: 4. Set your preferences for GPU, CPU and Usage type under the budget slider.
autoSection.step4Note: Make sure to check the "Include Orderable Items" to also use components that are not in store, but can be ordered.
autoSection.step5: 5. Once your budget is set, click the generate button to automatically complete your build.
autoSection.step5Note: Budget also affects component allocation. For example, lower-budget builds (e.g. under 500€) may exclude a GPU and instead select a CPU with integrated graphics. The same applies to components like CPU coolers. <link>See all budget allocations below</link>.
autoSection.step6: 6. After generating a build we recommend checking the compatability for the build manually before purchasing any of the items.
autoSection.step6Note: The HDD component is not automatically included, as it is optional. If the CPU comes with a built-in cooler and the budget is limited, a separate CPU cooler may also be treated as optional.

savedSection.title: Managing Your Saved Builds
savedSection.step1: 1. Go to the <savedLink>Saved</savedLink> page by clicking the <savedButton>Saved</savedButton> button in the navigation bar.
savedSection.step2: 2. Locate the build list and select the build you want to view.
savedSection.step3: 3. Click on any component to view its details and description below.
savedSection.step4: 4. To view a component in store, click the <seeInStore>See In Store</seeInStore> button in the description or the <buy>Buy</buy> button next to the component in the list.
savedSection.editStep1: 1. Click the <edit>Edit</edit> button next to your build name.
savedSection.editStep2: 2. Update the fields as needed. You can leave the notes field empty if you prefer.
savedSection.editStep3: 3. Click <save>Save</save> to apply your changes or <cancel>Cancel</cancel> to discard them.
savedSection.continueStep1: 1. Click the <continueBuild>Continue Build</continueBuild> button to resume editing your build from where you left off. You will be redirected to the build page with your current configuration.
savedSection.deleteStep1: 1. Click the <deleteBuild>Delete Build</deleteBuild> button (or the delete option in the build info panel) to remove the selected build.
savedSection.deleteStep2: 2. A confirmation popup will appear. Confirm to permanently delete the build.
savedSection.publishStep1: 1. Press on the button under components to publish your build. A popup window will show up to confirm changing builds visibility.
savedSection.publishStep2: 2. Once the build is public, you can see it in the Shared section and you can private it by following the first step again on the same build.
